replies.author: @RoamPlanet-4K

replies.text: Watching such high-quality footage makes us realize how wondrous and precious our natural world is. Thank you to the production team for putting so much heart into creating such great value for the community. May everyone watching this video right now be blessed with peace, good health, and always find beautiful moments filled with positive energy in your daily life!

replies.author: @jasondashney
replies.text: @GabbyCash if there's nothing political about it, then why is there such a crisp dividing line politically between people who worry greatly about it and those who don't? There's very little overlap in that venn diagram.  And I also say political because their are untold numbers of political regulations because of it. The best example is this nonsense about air conditioners in the UK. Heat wave that's literally killing people and they tell you to not use your air conditioner because of the electricity use, while at the same time, they try to push people into electric cars. And even worse than that, they want heat pumps for the winter, even though heat pumps are literally just reversed air-conditioning. The governments are involved and are diametrically opposed, which makes it political. Eye care deeply for the environment, but I couldn't give one iota of a crap about "climate change". I think our obsession with it hurts the environment big time. We ignore the actual real problems.  Seven rivers contribute almost all of the plastic to the ocean, but you didn't see Greta worrying about that, did you?  Of course not.  The other big tell that it's all political and performative is that nobody cared that the Paris Accord was a waste of money and wouldn't accomplish the goals.  The modern environmental movement doesn't give a damn if money is spent effectively, with measurable goals. They only cared to virtue signal that their "fighting climate change". Nobody who actually cares about the environment, takes the environmental movement seriously anymore.  Environmentalist should've been scrutinizing every aspect of that agreement to make sure it was efficient and going to help.  Instead, you had politicians flying across the world on private jets, and that one that stage that performance where she literally wrote a bicycle for the last couple hundred yards as a PR stunt. It's so gross and performative.

replies.author: @GabbyCash
replies.text: @jasondashneythere’s NOTHING political about global warming. To say so is incredibly ignorant. It is very real. Consider yourselves geographically lucky (for now)  for not being on the receiving end it.

replies.author: @jasondashney
replies.text: Yes, I was excited to watch this but right away they start in with the climate change nonsense. I don't know why everybody feel to go political. Just discuss the nature as it is.  The irony is that increased CO2 is great for the Amazon. The world is greening.  I'm gonna give it a few more minutes and if that's the last, I hear of it, then I'm excited to watch the rest. If they go political again, I'm out.

comments.text: The entire Amazon was once under cultivation by Native Americans. The newest through canopy radar technology has detected hundreds of signs of cities and civilizations, including large pyramids and stone structures. These people numbered in the millions until the arrival of Europeans with the diseases that largely destroyed the populations of both north and South America civilizations.
